title: Global Grain Trade Forecast Raised Yet Again
section_path: Global Grain Trade Forecast Raised Yet Again
section_path_text: Global Grain Trade Forecast Raised Yet Again
summary:
Main
By Jeffrey Landsberg
As we discussed in Commodore Research's most recent Weekly Executive Report, The United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) has released its latest forecast for the current 2025/26 season and is now predicting that global coarse grain, wheat, soybean, and soymeal exports will total 737.6 million tons. This is 3.5 million tons (1%) more than was predicted a month ago and would mark a year-on-year increase of 33 million tons (5%). Significant is that grain trade expectations continue to become more bullish.
